---
title: "CORE Demonstrator"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

## Interactive CORE Demonstrator

The CORE Demonstrator provides an interactive environment for exploring the methodology and workflow implemented by CORE.

The current demonstrator includes:

- candidate generation;
- confidence scoring;
- review queue management;
- expert validation;
- review history display;
- export of reviewed decisions.

The objective of the demonstrator is not to replace the full CORE framework, but to provide researchers with a transparent and accessible environment for understanding the methodological principles underlying the project.

---

## Launch Demonstrator

::: {.callout-tip}
## Live Demonstrator

The public demonstrator will become available following deployment of the first public release.

**Current status:** Under development.
:::

---

## Planned Capabilities

Future versions of the demonstrator are expected to support:

- upload of employer datasets;
- upload of reference datasets;
- interactive review workflows;
- diagnostics and explainability outputs;
- public demonstration workflows;
- benchmarking examples.

---

## Relationship with the CORE Framework

The demonstrator represents an interactive interface built on top of the methodological architecture implemented within CORE.

It therefore provides an accessible introduction to:

- the Entity Resolution Engine;
- the Review Queue;
- the Learning Layer;
- validated decision preservation.

Researchers interested in the full framework should consult the documentation website and GitHub repository.

---

## Current Development Status

The demonstrator currently supports:

- demonstration mode using bundled example datasets;
- research mode using user-provided datasets;
- review queue interaction;
- diagnostics inspection;
- validated review history display.

Public deployment is planned as part of the CORE v1.0 release roadmap.
